QF.id: XC Investment Products and Market Developments_3

question.text: Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) are categorized into how many categories under SEBI AIF Regulations, 2012?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI AIF Regulations 2012 classify AIFs into three categories: Category I (social venture, infrastructure, SME, VC funds), Category II (private equity, debt, fund of funds — residual category), and Category III (hedge funds, PIPE funds). **The Trap:** There is no Category IV or V. Some candidates confuse AIF categories with sub-categories within each category. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A Chapter 13 covers AIF overview. Series XIX-A/B/C/D/E are dedicated AIF exams with deep category analysis. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Category III AIFs can employ leverage and engage in short-selling, making them fundamentally different from Category I and II funds — ensure client suitability is thoroughly assessed for Category III.
